title: Fast Local Feedback, Hooks, and Developer Experience · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Create fast local gates that match CI without blocking ordinary iteration. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a one-command local verification workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Expose stable format, lint, type, test, and verify commands independent of tool internals.
Run fast deterministic changed-file checks in local hooks and keep heavier checks elsewhere.
Treat local hooks as convenience while CI remains authoritative and bypasses stay visible.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Command Interface · changed case

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: gates local hooks. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: gates local hooks. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Command Interface without dropping earlier contracts?

content.text: Expose stable format, lint, type, test, and verify commands independent of tool internals. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Feedback Quality applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Make failures concise, reproducible locally, and linked to remediation.
